get.description: List all device tasks matching criteria.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Limit for pagination purposes.

If unspecified or 0, the default value is 50. The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
Page token of the current page.

If not given or "", the first page is returned.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

post.description:
Create a [device task].

There is one [device task template] for every unique `display_name`, description, and `action` combination.
This template inherits `display_name`, `description` and `action` from the `task` in the request.

The created task requires to have at least one trigger set. Otherwise, the call fails.

get.description:
List task history - all the runs of the task.

For the sake of forensics, the [task run]s are listed collectively, not as children of their parent task. Information about task runs might easily survive their parent task.

> **Info:**
> Modeled based on [Jobs: Executions and results](https://google.aip.dev/152#executions-and-results) with ESET specific vocabulary.

parameters.name: taskUuid
parameters.description:
Include only runs of specific task. Mandatory.

type: DeviceTask

parameters.name: deviceUuid
parameters.description:
Include only runs on specific device (if filled).

type: device_management/v1/Device

parameters.name: listOnlyLastRuns
parameters.description: If true, the result will only contain the latest runs per device.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Limit for pagination purposes.

If unspecified or 0, the default value is 50. The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

triggers.description:
List of triggers that replace current triggers.

Cannot be empty.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

name.description:
Name of the function to execute.

GRPC/ERPC function names in the form of package.ServiceName/RpcName

For example:
- `eset.dotnod.scan_management.v1.ScanManager/CreateScan`
- `eset.dotnod.power_management.v1.PowerManager/RegisterAction`
- `eset.dotnod.network_access_protection.v1.NetworkAccessProtection/StartNetworkIsolation`

Actions outside .NOD are available as documented at [ESET Connect Online Help](https://help.eset.com/eset_connect/en-US/automation.html).

v1Action.description: Descriptor of the call to make.

assetGroupUuid.description:
Specifies in which asset group the task should be created.

If empty or null, the asset group will be assigned automatically.

type: asset_management.v1.Group

triggers.description:
Specifies *when* to run. What causes the task to be executed.

Cannot be empty.

uuid.description:
Unique identifier of the entity.

Must be collision-free - two identifiers created anywhere in the world must not collide within the entity parent scope. Unless a member of aggregate, the entity scope is always global.

Although most of the times compliant with [RFC 4122: A Universally Unique IDentifier (UUID) URN Namespace](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4122), do not rely on it being a RFC UUID. Treat it as an opaque identifier.

RFC UUID can be recognized by being formatted according to the template xxxxxxxx-xxxx-Mxxx-Nxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx, as explained on [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier#Format).

UUID is used for referencing an entity, even across domains.

Example: "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000"

createTime.description: The creation timestamp of an entity.

versionId.description:
Identifier of entity version. Every change to internal state of the entity leads to increase of this number. Every time this number changes, `update_time` must change as well.

[Version id] also determines whether one version is more recent than another. More recent versions have higher numbers.

> **Info:**
> Similar concepts are 
> - change sequence number (CSN)
> - version_id

> **Info:**
> Modeled after: https://google.aip.dev/162
>
> `revision_create_time` is delivered by `update_time` of https://google.aip.dev/148#timestamps

updateTime.description:
Represents the timestamp when the resource was most recently updated.  Any change to the resource made by users (create/update/delete) must update this value; changes to a resource made internally by the service should refresh this value unless specified otherwise on entity level.

> **Info:**
> Modeled after: https://google.aip.dev/148#timestamps

v1DeviceTask.description:
Describes a device task - a task to be executed on the device where some endpoint protection platform runs. It can also be executed indirectly if the actual device uses some device hub (for example, IoT scenarios).

Device tasks are abstract containers for scheduled `actions`. If the scheduling information is absent, actions are scheduled for ASAP execution.

Every execution of a [device task] instantiates `DeviceTaskRun`. Device tasks can be scheduled to be executed repeatedly, which creates many `DeviceTaskRun` entities.

deviceUuid.description:
Device where the task is executed. For tasks assigned to a device group, there can be multiple task runs from each member of the group.

type: device_management.v1.Device

endTime.description:
Timestamp of run end.

Meaningful for finished and failed task runs.

taskUuid.description:
Reference to [Device task]. Required.

type: DeviceTask

uuid.description:
Unique identifier of the entity.

Must be collision-free - two identifiers created anywhere in the world must not collide within the entity parent scope. Unless a member of aggregate, the entity scope is always global.

Although most of the times compliant with [RFC 4122: A Universally Unique IDentifier (UUID) URN Namespace](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4122), do not rely on it being a RFC UUID. Treat it as an opaque identifier.

RFC UUID can be recognized by being formatted according to the template xxxxxxxx-xxxx-Mxxx-Nxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx, as explained on [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier#Format).

UUID is used for referencing an entity, even across domains.

Example: "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000"

v1DeviceTaskRun.description:
One run (an execution) of the [device task].

[Task run] inherits all of its properties from the corresponding [device task].

> **Info:**
> Identity of the [task run] is kept for one task + device combination + start_time combination.

v1DeviceTaskSource.description:
Possible sources of a task.

 - DEVICE_TASK_SOURCE_UNSPECIFIED: fallback
 - DEVICE_TASK_SOURCE_INTERNAL_AUTOMATION: The task was created by automation internal to the tenant. For example, some internal rule system.
 - DEVICE_TASK_SOURCE_EXTERNAL_AUTOMATION: The task was created by automation external to the tenant/company. For example, a rule system of a company hired to provide managed detection and response services.
 - DEVICE_TASK_SOURCE_INTERNAL_USER: The task was created by the user from within the tenant/company. For example, an internal security officer.
 - DEVICE_TASK_SOURCE_EXTERNAL_USER: The task was created by the user external to the tenant/company. For example, a security manager of a company hired to provide managed detection and response services.

deviceGroupsUuids.description:
Task can be assigned to groups of devices.

type: device_management.v1.DeviceGroup

devicesUuids.description:
Task can be assigned to individual devices, for example, if the task run failed on these devices.

type: device_management.v1.Device

taskRuns.description: List of [task run]s matching criteria from the request.

nextPageToken.description:
Page token of the next page.

Empty or "" for the last page.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

Page token of the next page.

Empty or "" for the last page.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

createTime.description:
When the manual trigger has been created. The task can only be triggered after this time.

Read-only.

expireTime.description:
Task is not triggered after this time. 

This is useful for the task to not be triggered on stale targets (for example, those turned on after long time of vacation).

v1ManualTrigger.description:
For manually triggered tasks, this trigger causes immediate execution in `as soon as possible` fashion.

> **Info:**
> Examples:
- [Triggers: Manual Trigger](https://tray.io/documentation/connectors/triggers/manual-trigger/)
- [Windows Services: Trigger Start](https://www.coretechnologies.com/blog/windows-services/trigger-start/)
- [Manual Trigger](https://www.nocrm.io/help/manual-trigger-101)

v1TaskRunStatus.title: Possible TaskRun states
v1TaskRunStatus.description:
- TASK_RUN_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: fallback
 - TASK_RUN_STATUS_QUEUED: The task run has been processed but it is not yet running on the target.
 - TASK_RUN_STATUS_RUNNING: The task is being executed on the target.
 - TASK_RUN_STATUS_FINISHED: The task execution finished successfully.
 - TASK_RUN_STATUS_FAILED: The task execution failed.
 - TASK_RUN_STATUS_SUPPRESSED: The task was not executed due to suppression policies.

Suppression might occur for certain task sources to prevent damaging results. For example, a rule system that would wipe the device as a response to threat detection.

v1Trigger.description: Encodes information about when to run the task.

Bearer.description: Type "Bearer " and then your API Token
Bearer.name: Authorization
